canto_title: The Encounter with the Spirits of the Arno Valley

scenes.summary: Two spirits observe Dante and remark on his presence among the living.
The first spirit addresses Dante, marveling at his grace and his movement between worlds.
Dante describes the river Arno, and the spirit correctly identifies it.
The second spirit questions why Dante was so vague about the river's origin.
The first spirit describes the degeneration of the Arno valley, from swine to wolves.
The spirit describes the presence of deceptive foxes in the deep valleys.
The spirit prophesies the arrival of a hunter who will devastate the valley's beasts.
The second spirit is visibly disturbed by the news of the coming destruction.
The first spirit reveals his identity as Guido, the Duke's man, and speaks of the envy that ruined him.
Guido laments the corruption and emptiness of his own family's legacy and lands.
Guido points to the political decline of specific leaders and towns in the Romagna region.
Guido weeps for the lost nobility and the destruction of famous historical figures and families.
Guido continues his lament, describing the death and disappearance of various noble lineages.
Overwhelmed by grief, Guido concludes his speech, preferring to weep rather than speak.
As Dante and Virgil walk in silence, a sudden, thunderous voice strikes them from the air.
The second spirit appears with a great noise, identifying herself as Aglauro.
Startled by the sudden appearance, Dante instinctively recoils from the spirit.
Aglauro warns Dante that his focus on heavenly beauties makes him vulnerable to earthly temptations.
